examples.title: Simple TCP Server Socket
examples.description: Basic TCP server that accepts client connections and echoes back received data
examples.code:
import socket

def start_server():
    # Create TCP socket
    server_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    
    # Bind to port 8888
    server_socket.bind(('127.0.0.1', 8888))
    
    # Listen for connections (max 5 queued)
    server_socket.listen(5)
    print("Server listening on port 8888")
    
    while True:
        # Accept incoming connection
        client_socket, client_address = server_socket.accept()
        print(f"Connection from {client_address}")
        
        # Receive data from client
        data = client_socket.recv(1024)
        print(f"Received: {data.decode()}")
        
        # Send response back to client
        response = "Message received by server!"
        client_socket.send(response.encode())
        
        # Close client connection
        client_socket.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    start_server()
examples.explanation: This Python code creates a TCP server socket that listens on port 8888. When a client connects, it receives data, prints it, and sends an acknowledgment response. The server uses AF_INET for IPv4 and SOCK_STREAM for TCP protocol.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) per connection for receiving/sending data

examples.title: Simple TCP Client Socket
examples.description: Client that connects to server and sends/receives messages
examples.code:
import socket

def start_client():
    # Create TCP socket
    client_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    
    # Connect to server at localhost port 8888
    client_socket.connect(('127.0.0.1', 8888))
    
    # Send message to server
    message = "Hello from client!"
    client_socket.send(message.encode())
    print(f"Sent: {message}")
    
    # Receive response from server
    response = client_socket.recv(1024)
    print(f"Received: {response.decode()}")
    
    # Close connection
    client_socket.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    start_client()
examples.explanation: This client code creates a TCP socket and connects to the server. It sends a message and waits for a response. The client must run after the server is running and listening for connections.

examples.title: TCP Server in Java
examples.description: Java implementation of TCP server using ServerSocket class
examples.explanation: This Java code uses ServerSocket class to create a listening socket on port 8080. When a client connects, it reads the message using BufferedReader and responds using PrintWriter. Exception handling is required for network I/O operations.

examples.title: HTTP Request Processing (Simplified)
examples.description: Pseudocode showing basic HTTP server request handling process
examples.code:
ALGORITHM HTTP_Server_Process
BEGIN
    // Server initialization
    CREATE server_socket ON port 80
    LISTEN for incoming connections
    
    WHILE true DO
        // Accept client connection
        client_socket = ACCEPT connection
        
        // Read HTTP request
        request = READ from client_socket
        
        // Parse request (method, URL, headers)
        method = PARSE_METHOD(request)
        url = PARSE_URL(request)
        
        // Route to appropriate handler
        IF method = "GET" THEN
            response = HANDLE_GET(url)
        ELSE IF method = "POST" THEN
            response = HANDLE_POST(request)
        ELSE
            response = CREATE_ERROR_RESPONSE(405)
        END IF
        
        // Send HTTP response
        SEND response TO client_socket
        
        // Close connection
        CLOSE client_socket
    END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how an HTTP server processes client requests. It accepts connections, reads the HTTP request, parses the method (GET/POST) and URL, routes to appropriate handlers, and sends back an HTTP response with status code and content.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) per request based on content size
